priorityScale.P0: Serious: plausible production compromise, public mutation surface, or integrity break; resolve before beta/production unless explicitly accepted.
priorityScale.P1: Concerning: material exposure or abuse path; reduce before beta when feasible and block production unless acknowledged.
priorityScale.P2: Risky: hardening, minimization, or defense-in-depth gap; track with a concrete owner and release decision.
priorityScale.P3: Warning: hygiene or monitoring issue; keep visible so future code review does not reintroduce it.

productionGatePolicy.summary: Production publish is blocked when a tracked security issue is open and not explicitly acknowledged, or when a tracked issue is marked resolved but is still observed in the production artifact.
productionGatePolicy.betaBehavior: Beta publish shows tracked issues as reminders only.

findings.title: Production bundle exposes harness and debug mutation APIs
findings.summary: The public production bundle exposes harness/debug globals capable of mutating gameplay state, clock control, scoring paths, replay state, audio capture, and leaderboard test state.
findings.nextAction: Add a production build token or lane guard that prevents harness/debug globals from being exported in production and beta unless a review lane explicitly enables them.
findings.doneDefinition: Production and beta artifact scans no longer find harness globals or force-write symbols, while local/dev harness checks still pass.
proposedPlan: Strip harness/debug APIs from production builds by default.
Keep harness exports only in dev/test lanes, or guard them behind a compile-time production false constant.
Add production artifact checks for window.__galagaHarness__, window.__platinumHarness__, and force-write harness flag names.
Move diagnostic state needed by public support into narrow read-only reports.

findings.title: Leaderboard write integrity depends on client-trusted score submission
findings.summary: Score submission is assembled in the browser and sent through the public Supabase client. The anon key is expected to be public, but leaderboard integrity depends on strict RLS and server-side validation that the public artifact does not prove.
findings.nextAction: Capture the current Supabase RLS/policy state and classify what the browser can write; then decide whether beta can proceed with a documented leaderboard-integrity limitation.
findings.doneDefinition: A release artifact records the audited RLS policy, client writes cannot set trusted verification fields, and production score acceptance has a server-side validation or an explicit accepted-risk note.
findings.evidence: index.html contains public Supabase config and index.html contains client score insert
proposedPlan: Audit Supabase RLS for scores and profiles.
Require authenticated users to write only their own user_id and prevent client-controlled verified fields.
Move score acceptance to a server-side RPC or edge function that validates a signed run summary or replay evidence where practical.
Add a production release check that records the latest Supabase policy audit status before publishing.

findings.title: Production public artifact exposes reference audio assets
findings.summary: The public artifact still exposes reference-audio paths in generated docs, and historical live checks found clip URLs served publicly. Current local production evidence must distinguish public path references from actual bundled private audio bytes.
findings.nextAction: Split public guide references from private clip hrefs so production/beta can show evidence labels without publishing or linking private reference-audio paths.
findings.doneDefinition: Production and beta artifacts contain no reference-audio clip bytes and no public href/text path that invites fetching private reference clips; dev/local review keeps private provisioning explicit and ignored.
proposedPlan: Confirm which reference-audio clips are private, licensed, or production-safe.
Remove private reference-audio files from production and beta public artifacts.
Keep local/dev reference-audio provisioning through ignored local assets or private artifact bootstrap only.
Add artifact-boundary checks that fail production if dist/production references or contains private reference-audio paths.

findings.title: Hosted production lacks explicit browser security headers
findings.summary: The live GitHub Pages response did not expose obvious hardening headers such as Content-Security-Policy, frame-ancestors or X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, or HSTS.
findings.nextAction: Run a current hosted-header capture and determine whether a meta CSP is feasible without breaking the inline bundle.
findings.doneDefinition: The active host emits documented hardening headers or the app ships the strictest feasible meta policy with explicit residual GitHub Pages limitations.
findings.evidence: Manual host-header review remains required for GitHub Pages or the active production host.
proposedPlan: Decide whether to keep GitHub Pages or front it with a host that can set response headers.
Add CSP, frame-ancestors, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, and X-Content-Type-Options where the host permits.
If staying on GitHub Pages, add the strictest feasible meta CSP and document the residual limits.
Track any required unsafe-inline exceptions and remove them as build packaging improves.

findings.title: Production build metadata exposes test pilot emails and internal release context
findings.summary: Production build-info and bundle metadata include non-production test pilot emails and detailed commit/release state. This is not a secret leak by itself, but it gives unnecessary phishing and reconnaissance targets.
findings.nextAction: Sanitize production build-info and production JS token injection so non-production pilot emails and user ids are omitted outside dev/beta review lanes.
findings.doneDefinition: Production build-info and production index contain no non-production test pilot emails/user ids, while dev/beta/local auth review still has the needed test-pilot configuration.
proposedPlan: Remove non-production test pilot emails from production build-info and production JS.
Keep only public-safe release metadata in production build-info.
Move internal promotion lineage and test account data to private release artifacts.
Add metadata minimization checks to production publish preflight.

findings.title: Production bundle mixes dev, beta, and production account/test logic
findings.summary: Production code contains non-production test account rules, harness bypass concepts, and force-write controls. Even when guarded, this expands public attack surface and makes production security reasoning harder.
findings.nextAction: Introduce lane-specific build constants so production strips or hard-disables non-production account/test branches instead of carrying all lane logic in the public bundle.
findings.doneDefinition: Production bundle scans no longer find dev/test-only lane symbols unless they are documented as inert public support text; dev/beta behavior remains covered by harnesses.
proposedPlan: Split lane-specific dev/test controls from production runtime at build time.
Keep production account and leaderboard logic minimal and explicit.
Add a production bundle scan for dev/test-only symbols.
Document any intentionally retained diagnostics as read-only support surfaces.
